key_events: Absorbent years narrated: Oklahoma City bombing, Plano heroin crisis, Columbine, Greg's job instability, 9/11
Agency threshold reached at age 8 — Drew's 'competence as security' orientation crystallizes
Ages 8-11 compressed: helping at home, reading and building with Nana Carol, thriving at school, handling Kyle Briggs without flinching, friendship with Connor beginning to drift
psychological_changes: Attachment formed: secure with anxious undertone (Karen as primary)
Emotional inhibition schema formed (age 4-6): channels feelings into productivity
Self-sacrifice schema formed (age 8-9): measures worth by usefulness to others
Values hierarchy emerging: security, benevolence, self-direction, achievement
Self-concept crystallizing: 'I am someone who figures things out'
open_threads: Middle school transition — identity consolidation inflection point approaching
Connor friendship drifting as interests diverge
Drew's competence orientation untested against social hierarchy that values performance over substance
Emotional inhibition schema unchallenged — Drew hasn't yet faced a situation requiring emotional vulnerability
State history architecture unresolved — snapshot approach in place but needs formal design
player_intention: Retroactive wisdom — exploring what they would have done differently with adult perspective and discipline
